title: Open Enterprise AI Is Explicitly Being Framed as Multi-Provider and Composable
intent: Support a build-versus-buy strategy centered on composability rather than proprietary platform dependence.

source.title: LF AI & Data Foundation Launches Open Platform for Enterprise AI (OPEA) for Groundbreaking Enterprise AI Collaboration

fact.primary_stat: On April 16, 2024, OPEA was launched as a Linux Foundation sandbox project for open, multi-provider, composable GenAI systems.
supporting_stats: The launch announcement named a broad coalition including Cloudera, Hugging Face, Intel, MariaDB Foundation, MinIO, Qdrant, Red Hat, SAS, and others.
OPEA said its goal was to enable flexible and scalable enterprise GenAI systems using the best open source innovation across the ecosystem.

business_relevance: Composable enterprise AI is being formalized at ecosystem level, not just in isolated engineering teams.
Multi-provider design reduces dependency on a single model host, vector store, or orchestration vendor.
dzmitryi_commentary: This supports the positioning I want: help companies design the business layer and operating model for agents while keeping the technical stack swappable. In practice, that means side-by-side services, explicit capability boundaries, and a small set of contracts for search, memory, routing, and action execution.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Support a build-versus-buy strategy centered on composability rather than proprietary platform dependence.
